title: goodenia gloeophylla
intro: Goodenia gloeophylla, also known as the yellow butterfly bush, is a flowering plant native to Australia that belongs to the Goodenia genus of flowering plants in the family Goodeniaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is used to treat a range of ailments including colds, flu, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of Goodenia gloeophylla include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
